title: CLAUDE.md
text: file provides context Claude Code working ifc R package.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: ifc implements International Fixed Calendar vctrs-based S3 class (ifc_date). IFC 13 months 28 days , plus two intercalary days: Year Day (end every year) Leap Day (IFC June 28 leap years). every month starts Sunday ends Saturday, weekday date fully deterministic day--month.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Tests live tests/testthat/. source file corresponding test file: Run tests: Run single file:
code: tests/testthat/test-math.R tests/testthat/test-constructors.R tests/testthat/test-accessors.R tests/testthat/test-arithmetic.R tests/testthat/test-coerce.R tests/testthat/test-format.R tests/testthat/test-compat-lubridate.R devtools::test() testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-math.R")

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Integer-backed: ifc_date wraps integer days since Unix epoch (1970-01-01), identical base R Date. makes arithmetic Gregorian round-trips trivial. Gregorian bridge: calendar math goes DOY (day--year). epoch_to_ydoy() extracts year + DOY integer using format(Date,   "%Y"/%j"). doy_to_ifc() converts DOY IFC month/day collapsing Leap Day gap: doy_adj <- doy - (is_leap & doy > 169L). Special days: Year Day Leap Day month = NA day = NA. is_year_day is_leap_day logical flags distinguish . vctrs double-dispatch: vec_arith.ifc_date dispatches y via UseMethod("vec_arith.ifc_date", y). reverse direction vec_arith.numeric.ifc_date requires importFrom(vctrs, vec_arith.numeric) namespace (auto-imported). lubridate compat: Registered via vctrs::s3_register() .onLoad() rather NAMESPACE, keeping lubridate Suggests .

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Run devtools::document() regenerate NAMESPACE roxygen2 tags. ## usethis namespace: start/end block ifc-package.R must broken across lines use_lifecycle() usethis helpers. vec_arith.numeric vec_ptype2.Date must explicitly listed @importFrom vctrs — auto-importable.

title: Contributing to ifc
text: want make bigger change, ’s good idea first file issue make sure someone team agrees ’s needed. ’ve found bug, please file issue illustrates bug minimal reprex (also help write unit test, needed).

title: Contributing to ifc
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("k5cents/ifc", fork = TRUE). Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md (.e. just first header). Follow style described https://style.tidyverse.org/news.html.

title: Contributing to ifc
text: New code follow tidyverse style guide. can use styler package apply styles, please don’t restyle code nothing PR. use roxygen2, Markdown syntax, documentation. use testthat unit tests. Contributions test cases included easier accept.

code: @Manual{,   title = {ifc: International Fixed Calendar Date Class},   author = {Kiernan Nicholls},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://k5cents.github.io/ifc/}, }

title: International Fixed Calendar Date Class
text: ifc package implements International Fixed Calendar proper S3 vector class. IFC divides year 13 months exactly 28 days, Year Day appended year’s end (Leap Day IFC June 28 leap years). month always starts Sunday ends Saturday, day--week IFC date fully deterministic day--month number.

title: International Fixed Calendar Date Class
text: Install development version GitHub:

title: International Fixed Calendar Date Class
code: library(ifc) library(tibble)  # Convert from Gregorian ifc_date("2024-06-18") #> <ifc_date[1]> #> [1] "2024-07-01"  # Construct from IFC components (Sol 1, 2024) ifc_ymd(2024, 7, 1) #> <ifc_date[1]> #> [1] "2024-07-01"  # Intercalary days ifc_year_day(2024) #> <ifc_date[1]> #> [1] "2024 Year Day" ifc_leap_day(2024) #> <ifc_date[1]> #> [1] "2024 Leap Day" x <- ifc_ymd(2024, 7, 14)  ifc_year(x) #> [1] 2024 ifc_month(x)   # 7 = Sol #> [1] 7 ifc_day(x) #> [1] 14 ifc_wday(x, label = TRUE)  # deterministic: day 14 = Saturday #> [1] "Sat" format(x, "%B %d, %Y") #> [1] "Sol 14, 2024" format(x, "%a %B %d") #> [1] "Sat Sol 14" firsts <- seq(as.Date("2025/1/1"), by = "month", length.out = 12) tibble(   gregorian = firsts,   fixed = ifc_date(gregorian) ) #> # A tibble: 12 × 2 #>    gregorian        fixed #>    <date>           <ifc> #>  1 2025-01-01 2025 Jan 01 #>  2 2025-02-01 2025 Feb 04 #>  3 2025-03-01 2025 Mar 04 #>  4 2025-04-01 2025 Apr 07 #>  5 2025-05-01 2025 May 09 #>  6 2025-06-01 2025 Jun 12 #>  7 2025-07-01 2025 Sol 14 #>  8 2025-08-01 2025 Jul 17 #>  9 2025-09-01 2025 Aug 20 #> 10 2025-10-01 2025 Sep 22 #> 11 2025-11-01 2025 Oct 25 #> 12 2025-12-01 2025 Nov 27

what: Format an IFC date as a string — format.ifc_date
title: Format an IFC date as a string — format.ifc_date
text: Converts ifc_date character vector using format string IFC-aware tokens. Supported tokens:

title: Format an IFC date as a string — format.ifc_date
code: # S3 method for class 'ifc_date' format(   x,   format = "%Y-%m-%d",   special_fmt = c(year_day = "%Y Year Day", leap_day = "%Y Leap Day"),   ... )

title: Format an IFC date as a string — format.ifc_date
text: x ifc_date vector. format Character string format tokens. Default: "%Y-%m-%d". special_fmt Named character vector entries "year_day" "leap_day" giving format used special days. token available special_fmt %Y. Default: c(year_day = "%Y Year Day", leap_day = "%Y Leap Day"). ... Ignored; included S3 consistency.

title: Format an IFC date as a string — format.ifc_date
text: Character vector length x.

title: Format an IFC date as a string — format.ifc_date
text: Year Day Leap Day, month/day/weekday tokens produce empty string. rows always display special label using special_fmt argument.

title: Format an IFC date as a string — format.ifc_date

what: Format an IFC datetime as a string — format.ifc_datetime
title: Format an IFC datetime as a string — format.ifc_datetime
text: Converts ifc_datetime character vector using format string IFC-aware tokens. Supports tokens format.ifc_date() plus:

title: Format an IFC datetime as a string — format.ifc_datetime
code: # S3 method for class 'ifc_datetime' format(x, format = "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z", ...)

title: Format an IFC datetime as a string — format.ifc_datetime
text: x ifc_datetime vector. format Character string format tokens. Default: "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z". ... Ignored; included S3 consistency.

title: Format an IFC datetime as a string — format.ifc_datetime
text: Character vector length x.

title: Format an IFC datetime as a string — format.ifc_datetime
text: Year Day Leap Day, %B %b produce "Year Day" "Leap Day" respectively; %m, %d, %u, %, %produce empty strings. Time tokens always applied normally.

title: Format an IFC datetime as a string — format.ifc_datetime

what: ifc: International Fixed Calendar Date Class — ifc-package
title: ifc: International Fixed Calendar Date Class — ifc-package
text: ifc package implements International Fixed Calendar proper S3 vector class built vctrs framework. IFC divides year 13 months exactly 28 days, Year Day appended year's end (Leap Day IFC June 28 leap years). month always starts Sunday ends Saturday, day--week IFC date fully deterministic day--month number.

title: ifc: International Fixed Calendar Date Class — ifc-package
text: Maintainer: Kiernan Nicholls k5cents@gmail.com (ORCID) [copyright holder]

what: Extract components from an IFC date — ifc_accessors
title: Extract components from an IFC date — ifc_accessors
text: Extract components IFC date

title: Extract components from an IFC date — ifc_accessors
code: ifc_year(x)  ifc_month(x)  ifc_day(x)  ifc_yday(x)  ifc_wday(x, label = FALSE, abbr = TRUE)  ifc_week(x)  is_year_day(x)  is_leap_day(x)

title: Extract components from an IFC date — ifc_accessors
text: x ifc_date vector. label TRUE, return character label instead integer. abbr TRUE (label = TRUE), use abbreviated names.

title: Extract components from an IFC date — ifc_accessors
text: ifc_year(): integer year (Gregorian year). ifc_month(): integer month 1–13, NA Year Day / Leap Day. ifc_day(): integer day 1–28, NA Year Day / Leap Day. ifc_yday(): integer day year 1–366. ifc_wday(): weekday integer 1 (Sunday) – 7 (Saturday), NA Year Day / Leap Day. IFC, weekday fully determined (day - 1) %% 7 + 1. ifc_week(): integer week--year 1–52, NA Year Day / Leap Day. every IFC month exactly 4 weeks every year exactly 52 weeks, computed directly month day: (month - 1) * 4 + ceiling(day / 7). is_year_day(): logical, TRUE date Year Day. is_leap_day(): logical, TRUE date Leap Day.

title: Extract components from an IFC date — ifc_accessors
code: x <- ifc_ymd(2024, 7, 14) ifc_year(x) #> [1] 2024 ifc_month(x)  # 7 (Sol) #> [1] 7 ifc_day(x)  # 14 #> [1] 14 ifc_yday(x) #> [1] 183 ifc_wday(x)           # 7 (Saturday: day 14 = (14-1)%%7+1 = 7) #> [1] 7 ifc_wday(x, label = TRUE)   # "Sat" #> [1] "Sat" ifc_week(ifc_ymd(2024, 7, 14))   # 26: 6 full months (24 weeks) + week 2 of Sol #> [1] 26 ifc_week(ifc_year_day(2024))      # NA #> [1] NA is_year_day(ifc_year_day(2024))  # TRUE #> [1] TRUE is_leap_day(ifc_leap_day(2024))  # TRUE #> [1] TRUE

what: Add IFC calendar units to a date — ifc_add
title: Add IFC calendar units to a date — ifc_add
text: Add whole number weeks, months, years ifc_date vector using IFC calendar arithmetic. x n recycled common length.

title: Add IFC calendar units to a date — ifc_add
code: add_weeks(x, n)  add_months(x, n)  add_years(x, n)

title: Add IFC calendar units to a date — ifc_add
text: x ifc_date vector. n Integer number units add (may negative).

title: Add IFC calendar units to a date — ifc_add
text: ifc_date vector.

title: Add IFC calendar units to a date — ifc_add
text: add_weeks(x, n): adds n * 7L days (always exact). add_months(x, n): calendar arithmetic — preserves IFC day--month advances month n, wrapping across year boundaries. correctly handles June/Sol boundary leap years. add_years(x, n): calendar arithmetic — preserves IFC month day, advancing year n. Leap Day inputs, result Leap Day target year; non-leap target year produces error. Negative n moves backwards.

title: Add IFC calendar units to a date — ifc_add

title: Convert to an IFC datetime — ifc_datetime
Convert to an IFC datetime — ifc_datetime
text: x datetime-like object. tz Time zone string (e.g. "UTC", "America/New_York"). POSIXct input tz supplied, defaults timezone already stored x. ifc_date input, datetime placed midnight tz.

title: Convert to an IFC datetime — ifc_datetime
text: ifc_datetime vector.

title: Convert to an IFC datetime — ifc_datetime
code: ifc_datetime("2024-07-14 09:30:00", tz = "America/New_York") #> <ifc_datetime[1] tz=America/New_York> #> [1] "2024-07-27 09:30:00 EDT" ifc_datetime(ifc_ymd(2024, 7, 14), tz = "UTC") #> <ifc_datetime[1] tz=UTC> #> [1] "2024-07-14 00:00:00 UTC" ifc_datetime(Sys.time()) #> <ifc_datetime[1] tz=UTC> #> [1] "2026-03-18 22:49:40 UTC"

title: Current date-time in the IFC — ifc_now
Current date-time in the IFC — ifc_now
text: tz Time zone string. Defaults "UTC".

title: Current date-time in the IFC — ifc_now
text: ifc_datetime length 1 representing right now.

title: Current date-time in the IFC — ifc_now
code: ifc_now() #> <ifc_datetime[1] tz=UTC> #> [1] "2026-03-18 22:49:40 UTC" ifc_now("America/New_York") #> <ifc_datetime[1] tz=America/New_York> #> [1] "2026-03-18 18:49:40 EDT"

title: Parse IFC date strings — ifc_parse
text: Converts character strings ifc_date objects using format string IFC-aware tokens. default format "%Y-%m-%d" matches output format.ifc_date(), making format(x) |> ifc_parse(x) exact round-trip ifc_date values.

title: Parse IFC date strings — ifc_parse
Parse IFC date strings — ifc_parse
text: x Character vector IFC date strings. format Format string. Default: "%Y-%m-%d".

title: Parse IFC date strings — ifc_parse
text: ifc_date vector length x.

title: Parse IFC date strings — ifc_parse
text: Intercalary days always recognised canonical strings ("YYYY Year Day" "YYYY Leap Day") regardless format. Supported tokens (set format.ifc_date()):

title: Parse IFC date strings — ifc_parse
code: ifc_parse("2024-07-14")                    # IFC Sol 14, 2024 #> <ifc_date[1]> #> [1] "2024-07-14" ifc_parse("Sol 14, 2024", "%B %d, %Y") #> <ifc_date[1]> #> [1] "2024-07-14" ifc_parse("2024 Year Day") #> <ifc_date[1]> #> [1] "2024 Year Day" ifc_parse("2024 Leap Day") #> <ifc_date[1]> #> [1] "2024 Leap Day" # round-trip x <- ifc_ymd(2024, 7, 14) identical(ifc_parse(format(x)), x) #> [1] TRUE

what: Round an IFC date to a unit boundary — ifc_round
title: Round an IFC date to a unit boundary — ifc_round
text: Round, floor, ceiling ifc_date nearest "week", "month", "year" boundary. IFC's fixed-length units make operations exact: every week 7 days, every month 28 days.

title: Round an IFC date to a unit boundary — ifc_round
code: ifc_floor(x, unit)  ifc_ceiling(x, unit)  ifc_round(x, unit)

title: Round an IFC date to a unit boundary — ifc_round
text: x ifc_date vector. unit string: "week", "month", "year".

title: Round an IFC date to a unit boundary — ifc_round
text: ifc_date vector length x.

title: Round an IFC date to a unit boundary — ifc_round
text: Boundary definitions: week: Sunday (day 1 week) starts 7-day period. month: day 1 IFC month. year: January 1 IFC year. Intercalary day behaviour: Year Day Leap Day always follow Saturday (day 28 month), week floor 7 days back (preceding Sunday) month floor 28 days back (day 1 preceding month). Ceiling always advances next regular boundary: Leap Day ceiling = Sol 1; Year Day ceiling = January 1 following year. ifc_round() picks whichever boundary closer. Ties (e.g. day 15 "month") round toward floor.

title: Round an IFC date to a unit boundary — ifc_round
code: x <- ifc_ymd(2024, 7, 14) ifc_floor(x, "week")    # Sol 8  (Sunday of the same week) #> <ifc_date[1]> #> [1] "2024-07-08" ifc_floor(x, "month")   # Sol 1 #> <ifc_date[1]> #> [1] "2024-07-01" ifc_floor(x, "year")    # Jan 1, 2024 #> <ifc_date[1]> #> [1] "2024-01-01" ifc_ceiling(x, "week")  # Sol 15 (next Sunday) #> <ifc_date[1]> #> [1] "2024-07-15" ifc_ceiling(x, "month") # Aug 1  (day 1 of next month) #> <ifc_date[1]> #> [1] "2024-08-01" ifc_ceiling(x, "year")  # Jan 1, 2025 #> <ifc_date[1]> #> [1] "2025-01-01" ifc_round(x, "week")    # Sol 15 (day 14 = Saturday, 1 day from Sol 15) #> <ifc_date[1]> #> [1] "2024-07-15" ifc_round(x, "month")   # Sol 1  (day 14 is in the first half; ties go to floor) #> <ifc_date[1]> #> [1] "2024-07-01" ifc_round(x, "year")    # Jan 1, 2024 #> <ifc_date[1]> #> [1] "2024-01-01"

what: Generate a sequence of IFC dates — ifc_seq
title: Generate a sequence of IFC dates — ifc_seq
text: Creates regular sequence ifc_date values using IFC-native units.

title: Generate a sequence of IFC dates — ifc_seq
code: ifc_seq(from, to = NULL, by, length.out = NULL)

title: Generate a sequence of IFC dates — ifc_seq
text: ifc_date length 1. start sequence. ifc_date length 1, NULL. inclusive end sequence (reached exactly evenly divisible; otherwise sequence stops overshooting). string: one "day", "week", "month", "year". length.positive integer giving desired sequence length, NULL.

title: Generate a sequence of IFC dates — ifc_seq
text: ifc_date vector.

title: Generate a sequence of IFC dates — ifc_seq
text: = "day" = "week" use fixed epoch offsets (1 7 days). = "month" uses calendar arithmetic: result preserves IFC day--month, advancing one month time wrapping across year boundaries (month 13 wraps month 1 next year). correctly skips Leap Day crossing June/Sol boundary leap year. = "year" similarly preserves IFC month day, incrementing year. Exactly one length.must supplied. Direction (ascending descending) inferred automatically. Year Day Leap Day included sequences whenever arithmetic naturally lands (e.g. stepping "day" across year-end pass Year Day).

title: Generate a sequence of IFC dates — ifc_seq

what: Create an IFC date from year, month, and day components — ifc_ymd
title: Create an IFC date from year, month, and day components — ifc_ymd
text: Construct ifc_date IFC calendar year, month (1–13), day (1–28). Arguments recycled common length.

title: Create an IFC date from year, month, and day components — ifc_ymd
Create an IFC date from year, month, and day components — ifc_ymd
text: year Integer. Calendar year (Gregorian year). month Integer 1–13. IFC month number (7 = Sol, 8 = July, ..., 13 = December). day Integer 1–28. Day IFC month.

title: Create an IFC date from year, month, and day components — ifc_ymd
text: ifc_date vector.

title: Create an IFC date from year, month, and day components — ifc_ymd
ifc 0.1.0
text: Initial release. ifc_date() converts Date, POSIXct, character ifc_date. ifc_ymd() constructs IFC year, month (1–13), day (1–28). ifc_year_day() ifc_leap_day() create two intercalary days. Accessors: ifc_year(), ifc_month(), ifc_day(), ifc_yday(), ifc_wday(), is_year_day(), is_leap_day(). format.ifc_date() supports strftime-style tokens including %B → month name (e.g. "Sol") deterministic %/%weekday tokens. Full vctrs pillar integration use tibble columns.
